news.title: Mangaluru: ₹135 crore tender floated for tech park under PPP model
news.para: Mangaluru, the 'Silicon Beach of India', is set to get its first purpose-built tech park. Karnataka State Electronics Development Corporation (KEONICS) has floated a Rs 135 crore tender for the project, which will be developed under a public-private partnership (PPP) model.

news.title: NGT directs MoEFCC to review environmental clearance for Gurugram housing project
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has stepped in, instructing the Union Environment Ministry to reassess the environmental clearance for a proposed housing project expansion in Gurugram. This decision arises from challenges brought forth by a resident welfare group.

news.title: No 40% circle rate hike now, but Ghaziabad tweaks shop rates
news.para: Ghaziabad administration has shelved a proposed 40% property circle rate hike, opting for minor technical corrections instead. The decision comes after a drop in property registrations, with officials aiming to avoid further revenue decline. Existing rates largely remain from 2024, with slight increases for small commercial properties.

news.title: Builders in Karnataka face massive fines for plan violations
news.para: As per the new rates, residential, industrial, and other non-commercial structures in Town Panchayat areas will pay ₹1,000 per sq metre, and commercial buildings will be fined ₹1,500 per sq metre.

news.title: Karnataka RERA to verify projects under new closure policy
news.para: Karnataka Rera is set to implement a project closure policy, mirroring Odisha's initiative to enhance transparency and accountability in real estate. This policy will ensure builders meet all statutory and service compliances before projects are officially closed, safeguarding homebuyers from fraud and delays.

news.title: Bhubaneswar: BDA upholds order directing defaulting homeowners to pay maintenance fees
news.para: Bhubaneswar Development Authority has ordered defaulting residents of a housing project to pay over Rs 2 lakh in maintenance fees within a month. The authority upheld its earlier decision, empowering the allottees' association to recover dues. This ruling follows a dispute where some occupants refused payment, claiming the project wasn't fully handed over.

news.title: Minor building deviations? You can now regularise up to 15% in Bengaluru
news.para: Karnataka government has allowed regularization of building plan deviations up to 15% across urban local bodies, excluding Greater Bengaluru Area for now. Owners can obtain revised plans by paying prescribed fines, ranging from Rs 1,000 to Rs 3,000 per square meter, depending on the violation type and jurisdiction. A similar provision for Bengaluru is under preparation.

news.title: Builders snap up Linking Road societies in Mumbai, offer flat owners upto ₹1 lakh per sq ft
news.para: Linking Road in Mumbai is seeing builders offer huge sums to acquire entire housing societies. These deals are for redeveloping the prime location into malls and commercial spaces. Residents are accepting these offers to move out. This trend is transforming the area into a major retail hub. Property values are soaring due to high commercial FSI.

news.title: DM orders mandatory tenant, employee verification in Jammu by November 6
news.para: In a recent move aimed at bolstering security in Jammu, officials have issued a directive requiring all property owners and business operators to submit information about their tenants and employees to the local police. This must be completed within a week using a designated declaration form.

news.title: Property registrations in Mumbai dips 14% to 11,200 in October 2025
news.para: Mumbai property registrations are projected to reach 11,200 in October 2025, with stamp duty collections estimated at ₹1,004 crore. This represents a dip from the previous year. However, year-to-date figures show growth in both registrations and revenue. Residential deals continue to dominate the market, with homes priced below ₹1 crore seeing increased demand.

news.title: Plumbing body urges use of low-flow fittings to save water
news.para: The Indian Plumbing Association is urging manufacturers and real estate developers in West Bengal to adopt low-flow fittings for smart water management, potentially saving 50% of water. They will present a four-point demand to the state government, including a mandate for low-flow fixtures and compulsory water audits for all buildings.

news.title: Rajasthan HC junks green compensation imposed on brick kiln owners
news.para: Rajasthan High Court in Jodhpur has quashed environmental compensation levied by the State Pollution Control Board on 56 brick kiln owners. The court ruled the board lacks legal authority to impose such fees, ordering refunds for collected amounts within six weeks. Future compensation can only be imposed after the government frames clear rules and regulations.

news.title: Fake flat allotment racket busted in Lucknow; five persons booked for forging documents
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is investigating a large-scale forgery and fraud case involving fake flat allotment letters. An FIR has been registered against five individuals for allegedly creating and circulating forged documents with counterfeit signatures of senior LDA officers. The scheme came to light when a beneficiary reported receiving fake allotment letters via WhatsApp.

news.title: SRA to map every slum structure in Mumbai by December 2025
news.para: Mumbai's Slum Rehabilitation Authority (SRA) is mapping all slum structures by December 2025, implementing digital innovations like a multilingual portal, blockchain approvals, and automated data collation. These reforms aim to expedite project approvals, enhance transparency, and ensure accountability in slum rehabilitation schemes.

news.title: Stilt-plus-four-floor aims to tame urban sprawl, but architects in Ludhiana wary of civic crisis
news.para: Punjab government introduces new building rules allowing stilt-plus-four floors in major cities. This aims to control unplanned growth and increase housing options. Developers welcome the changes for vertical expansion. However, experts warn of strain on civic infrastructure without upgrades. The policy seeks to optimize land use and potentially make housing more affordable.

news.title: The Phoenix Mills' profit rises 39% to ₹304 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Phoenix Mills Ltd has reported a significant 39 percent jump in its consolidated net profit. The company's net profit reached Rs 303.99 crore in the second quarter of this fiscal year. This growth was driven by a substantial increase in its total income. The company's total income rose to Rs 1,146.21 crore during the July-September period.

news.title: Experts call for adopting low-carbon building materials for a sustainable future
news.para: Experts at an IIT Bhubaneswar workshop emphasised the adoption of low-carbon building materials for a sustainable built environment in Odisha. They called for policies, research, and green entrepreneurship to accelerate this transition, highlighting the state's rapid urban expansion and infrastructure goals. Officials stressed the responsibility of engineers and policymakers in ensuring environmentally sound growth and supporting sustainable materials.

news.title: Tamil Nadu's land reforms department issues instructions for record maintenance
news.para: The land reforms department has issued a circular to district collectorates mandating improved record maintenance following criticism from the state information commission. Instructions cover classification, storage, and marking of documents to ensure easy accessibility and long life, addressing issues that led to RTI petition denials.

news.title: Chandigarh completes drone data collection for NAKSHA project
news.para: Chandigarh has successfully completed drone-based data collection for the NAKSHA project across 15 sectors and five villages, creating accurate digital base maps. The administration is now proceeding with data validation, integrating ownership records, and generating urban property cards, with a focus on citizen verification via a digital platform.

news.title: Overhaul housing index compilation method: Statistics ministry
news.para: India's statistics ministry is set to overhaul the housing index compilation for the Consumer Price Index (CPI). Rent data will now be collected monthly, and the index will cover both rural and urban areas. The move aims to create a more accurate reflection of housing costs by excluding government accommodations and focusing on actual rental market transactions.
